reasoning: The control CM-7 (5) focuses on ensuring that only authorized software is allowed, following an allow-by-exception model. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network policies that restrict ingress and egress traffic by default, allowing only specified exceptions. Additionally, admission controllers can enforce software whitelisting by ensuring only approved images or configurations are deployed. Pod security standards can be used to ensure pods have the least functionality necessary, aligning with the control's intent.
title: Least Functionality | Authorized Software — Allow-by-exception
